intent: Stop focus mode from entering GAMING mode and killing browsers when no Steam is running. The daemon was flapping in and out of GAMING mode every few seconds against a Steam that had been uninstalled since 2026-07-04.
scope: Non-goal: the Steam/enforcer coexistence conflict. A healthy enforcer keeps a headless Steam alive permanently, which focus mode would correctly read as GAMING and lock browsers out for good. Only relevant if Steam is reinstalled, and needs its own decision (GAMING = client alive vs GAMING = a game actually running).
Non-goal: reinstalling Steam. User chose to leave it uninstalled.
Companion fix in the separate steam-backlog-enforcer repo (commit d54df3a) removes the zombie source itself.
changes: get_running_processes() reads /proc/*/stat instead of /proc/*/comm and skips processes in state Z (zombie). Both cost one read per PID, so the poll loop stays fork-free, but stat also carries the process state.

Added _parse_stat(), which extracts (name, state) using rpartition(')') because a process name may itself contain spaces and ')' - splitting on whitespace would corrupt such names. Names are truncated to 15 chars in stat exactly as in comm, so STEAM_PATTERNS and STEAM_GAME_PREFIX match unchanged.

Root cause: a total gaming block on 2026-07-04 purged the Steam client; the enforcer kept trying to launch it via an orphaned /usr/local/bin/steam wrapper pointing at the deleted /usr/bin/steam. Each failed launch left an unreaped zombie still named 'steam', which this daemon counted as a running Steam.

verification.command: probe get_running_processes() against live /proc while a real steam zombie was present
verification.evidence: zombies on system now: ['steam'] | OLD (/proc/*/comm) sees 'steam': True | NEW (zombie-filtered) sees 'steam': False | is_steam_running(NEW): False | dropped by filter: ['steam']. Reproduced across 6 consecutive polls.

verification.command: non-regression: synthetic non-zombie process sets through is_steam_running()
verification.evidence: synthetic live 'steam' -> True; synthetic game 'steam_app_29687' -> True. Proves the filter skips zombies, not Steam.

'Focus Mode Daemon starting...' then 'Browser detected - entering BROWSING mode'. No 'Steam detected - entering GAMING mode' lines at all; browsers untouched. Before the fix the same command flapped GAMING on/off every ~15s.
Deployed copy was STALE (grep -c ZOMBIE_STATE = 0) because install_focus_mode_daemon.sh copies rather than symlinks. Reinstalled; deployed file now byte-identical to source.

verification.command: pre-commit run --files <this file + focus_mode_daemon.py> (in a clean worktree)
verification.evidence: Run from a temporary git worktree because the main tree carries unrelated uncommitted brother_printer work whose tests fail; with brother_printer at its committed state the suite is 340 passed.

risks: Reading /proc/*/stat instead of /proc/*/comm is a wider parse surface; mitigated by rpartition(')') handling names containing ')' or spaces, which naive whitespace splitting would break.
A process that is genuinely running is never skipped - only state Z is filtered - so real Steam and real games are still detected.
install_focus_mode_daemon.sh copies rather than symlinks, so editing the repo file alone does not change what runs. Any future change must be re-installed to /usr/local/bin/focus-mode-daemon.

The removed /usr/local/bin/steam wrapper carried GPU workaround flags: exec /usr/bin/steam -cef-disable-gpu -cef-disable-gpu-compositing "$@" . Restore them if a Steam reinstall brings back GPU trouble.
rollback: git revert the commit, then re-run: sudo install -m 755 focus_mode_daemon.py /usr/local/bin/focus-mode-daemon (reverting the repo file alone does NOT change the deployed daemon).
After rollback, expect focus mode to once again count zombie processes as running; verify with the probe that /proc/*/comm-based detection returns True for a 'steam' zombie.
The enforcer fix (steam-backlog-enforcer d54df3a) is independent and removes the zombie source, so rolling this back alone should not resurrect the symptom unless the enforcer is also reverted.
